---
title: "BZAN 542 - Hackathon"
author: "You"
date: "November 2025"
output: html_document
---

## Background

State Departments of Transportation use analytics to study traffic patterns, flow, congestion, and how weather conditions impact the number of cars on the roads.  

Some basic examples of traffic pattern analysis includes:

* Peak vs. Off-Peak Hours - Identify the busiest times of the day to optimize road maintenance schedules and adjust traffic management strategies.

* Weekday vs. Weekend Trends - Understand how traffic changes on weekends and holidays to plan for increased or decreased flow.

* Seasonal Trends - Analyze how traffic volume changes throughout the year to prepare for seasonal maintenance or staffing needs.

* Accident Correlation - If accident data is available, correlate crashes with specific weather conditions to enhance safety measures.

* Weather Impact - Determine if extreme heat or cold influences travel behavior, assess how weather affects average speeds and congestion levels to optimize speed limit adjustments, and identify reductions in traffic volume during adverse weather conditions to improve winter road maintenance and emergency response plans.

However, in this Hackathon, the goal will be to predict the number of cars traveling on a specific interstate in the midwest in the late winter and early spring months.  Knowing the number of cars on roads is quite important!

* Schedule repairs and lane closures during low-traffic periods to minimize disruptions.

* Adjust speed limits, ramp metering, and signage dynamically based on weather and congestion patterns.

* Provide better traffic and weather advisories to drivers based on real-time and predicted conditions.

* Use long-term trends to justify road expansions, additional lanes, or new traffic control measures.


## Data 

You are given information about the number of cars on the road during particular hours:

* `holiday` - if the day is holiday or not
* `temp` - the average temperature during that hour
* `rain_1h` - numeric amount in mm of rain that occurred in the hour
* `snow_1h` - numeric amount in mm of snow that occurred in the hour
* `clouds_all` - numeric percentage of cloud cover that occurred in the hour
* `weather_main` - short description of the weather during the hour
* `weather_descriptions` - longer description of the weather during the hour
* `date_time` - tells us which hour of the day the row corresponds to
* `traffic_volume` - number of cars on the interstate
* `ID` - row identifier

For this competition, you are predicting a numerical response (`traffic_volume`) and will be judged on the RMSE (typical error made by the model on kaggle holdout sample).

Unlike in other activities and homeworks, we'll forgo splitting the `TRAIN` dataframe in the .RData file into a separate training/holdout sample and will instead use the entirety of `TRAIN` to fit models, to estimate their generalization errors, and to submit predictions for the holdout sample stored on kaggle.

After making good data wrangling choices (also consider "engineering" new predictors), be sure to intelligently tune the models.  If you find the "optimal" parameter to be at the edge of the grid, probe further for a potentially better model! For example, if you were trying out nearest-neighbors models with k=1-15, and found the "best" value of k was 15, try out larger values of k like 20, 30, 50!


## Setup and short walkthrough

```{r}
#Recommend to load up these libraries
library(regclass)
library(tidymodels)
library(tidyverse)
library(forcats)
library(lubridate)
library(kknn)
library(klaR)
library(discrim)
library(yardstick)
library(ggplot2)
library(discrim)
library(doParallel)
library(vip)
library(DALEX)
library(DALEXtra)
library(pdp)
library(timeDate)
library(xgboost)
library(stacks)
library(h2o)

#Load in the data.  Can do this manually with Session, Load Workspace
load("BZAN542_FALL25_HACKATHON.RData") 
glimpse(KAGGLE)
glimpse(TRAIN)

#TRAIN - has the actual traffic volumes for each row
#KAGGLE - doesn't have the traffic volumes; you're submitted predictions for these rows and uploading to kaggle





#Since you DO need to do a little data preprocessing (handling the data), you'll need to stack the
#TRAIN and KAGGLE datasets

TRAFFIC <- rbind(TRAIN,KAGGLE) #stack the 2 datasets together

summary(TRAFFIC)

TRAFFIC %>%
  arrange(as.POSIXct(date_time)) %>%
  mutate(dt = as.POSIXct(date_time)) %>%
  mutate(diff_hours = difftime(dt, lag(dt), units = "hours")) %>%
  count(diff_hours)

# improved_pipeline_exact_names.R
# Assumes you have already or will run: load("BZAN542_FALL25_HACKATHON.RData")
# which provides TRAIN and KAGGLE data.frames (or tibbles).
# Script uses exact column names in your workspace:
# TRAIN, KAGGLE, TRAFFIC, date_time, traffic_volume, ID, holiday, temp, rain_1h, snow_1h, clouds_all, weather_main, weather_description

library(data.table)
library(lubridate)
library(rsample)
library(lightgbm)
library(Matrix)

# ------------------ 0) Prep: load data if needed ------------------
# Uncomment if you need to load the RData here:
# load("BZAN542_FALL25_HACKATHON.RData")

# Ensure TRAIN & KAGGLE exist
if (!exists("TRAIN") || !exists("KAGGLE")) stop("TRAIN and/or KAGGLE not found in workspace. Load BZAN542_FALL25_HACKATHON.RData first.")

setDT(TRAIN); setDT(KAGGLE)

# ------------------ 1) stack into TRAFFIC (use same name you used) ------------------
TRAFFIC <- rbindlist(list(TRAIN, KAGGLE), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

# ------------------ 2) convert date_time to POSIXct (preserve exact name) ------------------
# Your date_time examples look like "YYYY-mm-dd HH:MM:SS"
TRAFFIC[, date_time := ymd_hms(as.character(date_time), tz = "UTC")]
if (any(is.na(TRAFFIC$date_time))) {
  # fallback to ymd_hm if seconds missing
  TRAFFIC[is.na(date_time), date_time := ymd_hm(as.character(date_time), tz = "UTC")]
}
if (any(is.na(TRAFFIC$date_time))) stop("Some date_time could not be parsed. Check formats.")

# sort by time (important)
setorderv(TRAFFIC, "date_time")

# create index to restore order if needed
TRAFFIC[, row_id := .I]

# ------------------ 3) basic time features (exact names) ------------------
TRAFFIC[, hour := hour(date_time)]
TRAFFIC[, wday := wday(date_time) - 1L]  # 0..6 (Sunday..Saturday)
TRAFFIC[, month := month(date_time)]
TRAFFIC[, year := year(date_time)]
TRAFFIC[, doy := yday(date_time)]

# cyclic encodings
TRAFFIC[, sin_hour := sin(2*pi*hour/24)]
TRAFFIC[, cos_hour := cos(2*pi*hour/24)]
TRAFFIC[, sin_wk := sin(2*pi*wday/7)]
TRAFFIC[, cos_wk := cos(2*pi*wday/7)]

# keep holiday factor as-is (or convert to integer)
if (is.factor(TRAFFIC$holiday) || is.character(TRAFFIC$holiday)) {
  TRAFFIC[, holiday_code := as.integer(as.factor(as.character(holiday)))]
} else {
  TRAFFIC[, holiday_code := as.integer(holiday)]
}

# ------------------ 4) TIME-AWARE LAGS (Option B set) ------------------
# We'll create lags for: 1h,2h,3h,6h,12h,24h,48h,72h,168h
# TIME-AWARE LAGS (Option B set)
# TIME-AWARE LAGS (Option B set)

TRAFFIC[, .N, by = date_time][N > 1]
lookup[, .N, by = date_time][N > 1]

lags_h <- c(1,2,3,6,12,24,48,72,168)

# lookup: traffic_volume keyed by date_time


lookup_unique <- lookup[, .SD[.N], by = date_time]   # last row
setkey(lookup_unique, date_time)

for (h in lags_h) {
  newcol <- paste0("lag_", h, "h")
  TRAFFIC[, desired := date_time - dhours(h)]
  
  TRAFFIC[, (newcol) := lookup_unique[
    TRAFFIC, 
    on = .(date_time = desired), 
    x.traffic_volume
  ]]
  
  TRAFFIC[, desired := NULL]
}


# previous-observation (closest earlier timestamp irrespective of exact hour)
# Compute "prev_obs": immediate previous traffic_volume (for same ordered dataset)
TRAFFIC[, prev_obs := shift(traffic_volume, n = 1, type = "lag")]

# ------------------ 5) ROLLING AGGREGATES (time-windowed) ------------------
# Option B: rolling means for last 2h, 6h, 24h (time-aware windows)
windows_h <- c(2, 6, 24)

# convert vector to POSIXct for comparisons
times <- TRAFFIC$date_time
tv <- TRAFFIC$traffic_volume

# function to compute time-window mean for each row (only uses earlier rows)
rolling_mean_by_window <- function(times, values, window_hours) {
  n <- length(times)
  res <- numeric(n)
  for (i in seq_len(n)) {
    end_t <- times[i]
    start_t <- end_t - dhours(window_hours)
    # include only prior times strictly before end_t
    sel <- which(times > start_t & times < end_t & !is.na(values))
    if (length(sel) == 0) res[i] <- NA_real_ else res[i] <- mean(values[sel], na.rm=TRUE)
  }
  return(res)
}

# compute and add columns
for (w in windows_h) {
  nm <- paste0("roll_mean_", w, "h")
  TRAFFIC[, (nm) := rolling_mean_by_window(times = TRAFFIC$date_time, values = TRAFFIC$traffic_volume, window_hours = w)]
  nm2 <- paste0("roll_sd_", w, "h")
  TRAFFIC[, (nm2) := {
    v <- numeric(.N)
    for (i in seq_len(.N)) {
      end_t <- date_time[i]; start_t <- end_t - dhours(w)
      sel <- which(date_time > start_t & date_time < end_t & !is.na(traffic_volume))
      v[i] <- if (length(sel)==0) NA_real_ else sd(traffic_volume[sel], na.rm = TRUE)
    }
    v
  }]
}

# lightweight derived features
TRAFFIC[, pct_change_1h := (lag_1h - lag_2h)/ (lag_2h + 1e-9)]
TRAFFIC[, diff_1h_24h := (lag_1h - lag_24h)]

# ------------------ 6) prepare dataset for modeling ------------------
# split back to train and test by traffic_volume NA
train_dt <- TRAFFIC[!is.na(traffic_volume)]
test_dt  <- TRAFFIC[is.na(traffic_volume)]

# remove rows from training that are before first available lag if you want (optional)
# For now we keep them (LightGBM handles NA).

# select features - keep only numeric and low-cardinality converted factors
# We'll include:
base_feats <- c("temp","rain_1h","snow_1h","clouds_all",
                "hour","wday","month","year","doy",
                "sin_hour","cos_hour","sin_wk","cos_wk",
                "holiday_code",
                paste0("lag_", lags_h, "h"),
                "prev_obs",
                paste0("roll_mean_", windows_h, "h"),
                paste0("roll_sd_", windows_h, "h"),
                "pct_change_1h","diff_1h_24h"
)

# ensure features exist, drop missing ones gracefully
feats <- intersect(base_feats, names(TRAFFIC))
cat("Using", length(feats), "features.\n")

# convert factor weather_main / weather_description to integers (low-card)
if ("weather_main" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  train_dt[, weather_main_code := as.integer(as.factor(as.character(weather_main)))]
  test_dt[,  weather_main_code := as.integer(as.factor(as.character(weather_main)))]
  feats <- c(feats, "weather_main_code")
}
if ("weather_description" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  train_dt[, weather_desc_code := as.integer(as.factor(as.character(weather_description)))]
  test_dt[,  weather_desc_code := as.integer(as.factor(as.character(weather_description)))]
  feats <- c(feats, "weather_desc_code")
}

# finalize features (de-duplicate)
feats <- unique(feats)

# Replace Inf with NA
for (f in feats) {
  set(train_dt, which(is.infinite(train_dt[[f]])), f, NA)
  set(test_dt,  which(is.infinite(test_dt[[f]])), f, NA)
}

# ------------------ 7) time-series CV: rolling_origin ------------------
# Make sure train_dt is ordered by date_time
setorderv(train_dt, "date_time")

n <- nrow(train_dt)
initial_prop <- 0.7
assess_prop <- 0.1
initial <- floor(n * initial_prop)
assess  <- floor(n * assess_prop)

ro <- rolling_origin(train_dt, initial = initial, assess = assess, cumulative = TRUE)
# limit folds for speed on laptop
max_folds <- min(length(ro$splits), 5)
ro$splits <- ro$splits[seq_len(max_folds)]
cat("Using", length(ro$splits), "rolling CV folds.\n")

# ------------------ 7) time-series CV: rolling_origin ------------------
# Make sure train_dt is ordered by date_time
setorderv(train_dt, "date_time")

n <- nrow(train_dt)
initial_prop <- 0.7
assess_prop <- 0.1
initial <- floor(n * initial_prop)
assess  <- floor(n * assess_prop)

ro <- rolling_origin(train_dt, initial = initial, assess = assess, cumulative = TRUE)

# Limit folds for speed on laptop
# Extract the splits and limit them
max_folds <- min(nrow(ro), 5)  # ro is a tibble, use nrow() instead
ro <- ro[seq_len(max_folds), ]  # Subset the tibble directly, not the $splits component

cat("Using", nrow(ro), "rolling CV folds.\n")

# Later, when iterating, use:
for (i in seq_len(nrow(ro))) {
  split <- ro$splits[[i]]  # Extract individual split
  cat("Training fold", i, "...\n")
  # ... rest of your code
}

# ------------------ 8) LightGBM training (fold-wise) ------------------
# prepare params tuned for lower memory machines
lgb_params <- list(
  objective = "regression",
  metric = "rmse",
  learning_rate = 0.05,
  num_leaves = 31,
  min_data_in_leaf = 20,
  feature_fraction = 0.8,
  bagging_fraction = 0.8,
  bagging_freq = 5,
  verbosity = -1
)

nrounds <- 3000
early_stopping_rounds <- 50

oof_preds <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(train_dt))
test_preds_sum <- rep(0, nrow(test_dt))
models_list <- list()
fold <- 1

for (split in ro$splits) {
  cat("Training fold", fold, "...\n")
  tr_idx <- analysis(split)
  va_idx <- assessment(split)
  dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(tr_idx[, ..feats]), label = tr_idx$traffic_volume)
  dval   <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), label = va_idx$traffic_volume)
  model <- lgb.train(params = lgb_params,
                     data = dtrain,
                     valids = list(valid = dval),
                     nrounds = nrounds,
                     early_stopping_rounds = early_stopping_rounds,
                     verbose = 1)
  models_list[[fold]] <- model

  # predict validation -> fill OOF by matching date_time (ensure alignment)
  pred_val <- predict(model, as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), n = model$best_iter)
  # find indices in train_dt to place preds:
  idx_in_train <- match(va_idx$row_id, train_dt$row_id)
  oof_preds[idx_in_train] <- pred_val

  # predict test and accumulate
  pred_test_fold <- predict(model, as.matrix(test_dt[, ..feats]), n = model$best_iter)
  test_preds_sum <- test_preds_sum + pred_test_fold

  fold <- fold + 1
  gc()
}

# ------------------ 8) LightGBM training (fold-wise) ------------------
# prepare params tuned for lower memory machines
lgb_params <- list(
  objective = "regression",
  metric = "rmse",
  learning_rate = 0.05,
  num_leaves = 31,
  min_data_in_leaf = 20,
  feature_fraction = 0.8,
  bagging_fraction = 0.8,
  bagging_freq = 5,
  verbosity = -1
)

nrounds <- 3000
early_stopping_rounds <- 50

oof_preds <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(train_dt))
test_preds_sum <- rep(0, nrow(test_dt))
models_list <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(ro))) {
  split <- ro$splits[[i]]
  cat("Training fold", i, "...\n")
  
  tr_idx <- analysis(split)
  va_idx <- assessment(split)
  
  dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(tr_idx[, ..feats]), label = tr_idx$traffic_volume)
  dval   <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), label = va_idx$traffic_volume)
  
  model <- lgb.train(params = lgb_params,
                     data = dtrain,
                     valids = list(valid = dval),
                     nrounds = nrounds,
                     early_stopping_rounds = early_stopping_rounds,
                     verbose = 1)
  
  models_list[[i]] <- model

  # predict validation -> fill OOF by matching date_time (ensure alignment)
  pred_val <- predict(model, as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), num_iteration = model$best_iter)
  
  # find indices in train_dt to place preds:
  idx_in_train <- match(va_idx$row_id, train_dt$row_id)
  oof_preds[idx_in_train] <- pred_val

  # predict test and accumulate
  pred_test_fold <- predict(model, as.matrix(test_dt[, ..feats]), num_iteration = model$best_iter)
  test_preds_sum <- test_preds_sum + pred_test_fold

  gc()
}

test_preds_avg <- test_preds_sum / length(models_list)

# compute OOF RMSE
oo <- which(!is.na(oof_preds))
oof_rmse <- sqrt(mean((oof_preds[oo] - train_dt$traffic_volume[oo])^2))
cat("OOF RMSE (time-aware rolling CV):", round(oof_rmse, 4), "\n")

test_preds_avg <- test_preds_sum / length(models_list)

# compute OOF RMSE
oo <- which(!is.na(oof_preds))
oof_rmse <- sqrt(mean((oof_preds[oo] - train_dt$traffic_volume[oo])^2))
cat("OOF RMSE (time-aware rolling CV):", round(oof_rmse, 4), "\n")

# ------------------ 9) Feature importance (last model) ------------------
if (length(models_list) > 0) {
  imp <- lgb.importance(models_list[[length(models_list)]])
  print(head(imp, 30))
  # save importance
  fwrite(as.data.table(imp), file = "feature_importance_exact_names.csv")
}

# ------------------ 10) prepare submission (use ID column exactly) ------------------
# We'll output submission with columns: ID, traffic_volume (as per original)
submission <- copy(test_dt[, .(ID)])
submission[, traffic_volume := test_preds_avg]

fwrite(submission, "submission_improved_exact_names.csv")
cat("Saved submission_improved_exact_names.csv with", nrow(submission), "rows.\n")

# Save models (optional)
saveRDS(models_list, file = "lgb_models_exact_names.rds")
cat("Pipeline finished.\n")


# IMPROVEMENTS for better RMSE:
# 1. Better hyperparameter tuning
# 2. More sophisticated lag features (lag interactions)
# 3. Better handling of missing values
# 4. Ensemble multiple models
# 5. Better feature engineering

# First, improve feature engineering BEFORE training

# Add interaction features
TRAFFIC[, lag_1h_x_hour := lag_1h * sin_hour]
TRAFFIC[, lag_1h_x_temp := lag_1h * temp]
TRAFFIC[, temp_x_hour := temp * sin_hour]
TRAFFIC[, rain_snow_interaction := rain_1h * snow_1h]

# Add more sophisticated rolling stats
TRAFFIC[, roll_max_6h := {
  v <- numeric(.N)
  for (i in seq_len(.N)) {
    end_t <- date_time[i]; start_t <- end_t - dhours(6)
    sel <- which(date_time > start_t & date_time < end_t & !is.na(traffic_volume))
    v[i] <- if (length(sel)==0) NA_real_ else max(traffic_volume[sel], na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  v
}]

TRAFFIC[, roll_min_6h := {
  v <- numeric(.N)
  for (i in seq_len(.N)) {
    end_t <- date_time[i]; start_t <- end_t - dhours(6)
    sel <- which(date_time > start_t & date_time < end_t & !is.na(traffic_volume))
    v[i] <- if (length(sel)==0) NA_real_ else min(traffic_volume[sel], na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  v
}]

# Add ratio features (handle division by zero)
TRAFFIC[, lag_1h_to_lag_24h := (lag_1h + 1) / (lag_24h + 1)]
TRAFFIC[, roll_mean_2h_to_6h := (roll_mean_2h + 1) / (roll_mean_6h + 1)]

# Split data again
train_dt <- TRAFFIC[!is.na(traffic_volume)]
test_dt  <- TRAFFIC[is.na(traffic_volume)]

# Updated feature list
base_feats <- c("temp","rain_1h","snow_1h","clouds_all",
                "hour","wday","month","year","doy",
                "sin_hour","cos_hour","sin_wk","cos_wk",
                "holiday_code",
                paste0("lag_", lags_h, "h"),
                "prev_obs",
                paste0("roll_mean_", windows_h, "h"),
                paste0("roll_sd_", windows_h, "h"),
                "roll_max_6h", "roll_min_6h",
                "pct_change_1h","diff_1h_24h",
                "lag_1h_x_hour", "lag_1h_x_temp", "temp_x_hour",
                "rain_snow_interaction", "lag_1h_to_lag_24h", "roll_mean_2h_to_6h"
)

feats <- intersect(base_feats, names(TRAFFIC))

# Weather encoding
if ("weather_main" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  train_dt[, weather_main_code := as.integer(as.factor(as.character(weather_main)))]
  test_dt[,  weather_main_code := as.integer(as.factor(as.character(weather_main)))]
  feats <- c(feats, "weather_main_code")
}

feats <- unique(feats)
 
# Handle NAs - impute with median instead of leaving as NA
for (f in feats) {
  set(train_dt, which(is.infinite(train_dt[[f]])), f, NA)
  set(test_dt,  which(is.infinite(test_dt[[f]])), f, NA)
  
  # Impute NAs with median from training data
  median_val <- median(train_dt[[f]], na.rm = TRUE)
  if (!is.na(median_val)) {
    set(train_dt, which(is.na(train_dt[[f]])), f, median_val)
    set(test_dt,  which(is.na(test_dt[[f]])), f, median_val)
  }
}

setorderv(train_dt, "date_time")

# Prepare CV with more folds for better estimates
n <- nrow(train_dt)
initial_prop <- 0.6
assess_prop <- 0.15
initial <- floor(n * initial_prop)
assess  <- floor(n * assess_prop)

ro <- rolling_origin(train_dt, initial = initial, assess = assess, cumulative = TRUE)
max_folds <- min(nrow(ro), 8)  # Increase to 8 folds
ro <- ro[seq_len(max_folds), ]

cat("Using", nrow(ro), "rolling CV folds.\n")

# BETTER hyperparameters - tuned for regression
lgb_params <- list(
  objective = "regression",
  metric = "rmse",
  learning_rate = 0.02,        # Lower learning rate (was 0.05)
  num_leaves = 63,              # Slightly deeper (was 31)
  min_data_in_leaf = 10,        # Lower minimum (was 20)
  feature_fraction = 0.7,       # More aggressive (was 0.8)
  bagging_fraction = 0.7,       # More aggressive (was 0.8)
  bagging_freq = 5,
  lambda_l2 = 1.0,              # Add regularization
  lambda_l1 = 0.5,              # Add regularization
  verbosity = -1
)

nrounds <- 5000             # Increase rounds (was 3000)
early_stopping_rounds <- 100 # Increase patience (was 50)

oof_preds <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(train_dt))
test_preds_sum <- rep(0, nrow(test_dt))
models_list <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(ro))) {
  split <- ro$splits[[i]]
  cat("Training fold", i, "of", nrow(ro), "...\n")
  
  tr_idx <- analysis(split)
  va_idx <- assessment(split)
  
  dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(tr_idx[, ..feats]), label = tr_idx$traffic_volume)
  dval   <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), label = va_idx$traffic_volume)
  
  model <- lgb.train(params = lgb_params,
                     data = dtrain,
                     valids = list(valid = dval),
                     nrounds = nrounds,
                     early_stopping_rounds = early_stopping_rounds,
                     verbose = 0)
  
  models_list[[i]] <- model

  pred_val <- predict(model, as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), num_iteration = model$best_iter)
  idx_in_train <- match(va_idx$row_id, train_dt$row_id)
  oof_preds[idx_in_train] <- pred_val

  pred_test_fold <- predict(model, as.matrix(test_dt[, ..feats]), num_iteration = model$best_iter)
  test_preds_sum <- test_preds_sum + pred_test_fold
  
  cat("  Best iteration:", model$best_iter, "\n")

  gc()
}

test_preds_avg <- test_preds_sum / length(models_list)

# Compute OOF RMSE
oo <- which(!is.na(oof_preds))
oof_rmse <- sqrt(mean((oof_preds[oo] - train_dt$traffic_volume[oo])^2))
cat("OOF RMSE:", round(oof_rmse, 4), "\n")

# Feature importance
if (length(models_list) > 0) {
  imp <- lgb.importance(models_list[[1]])
  cat("\nTop 20 Features:\n")
  print(head(imp, 20))
  fwrite(as.data.table(imp), file = "feature_importance_improved.csv")
}

# Prepare submission
submission <- copy(test_dt[, .(ID)])
submission[, traffic_volume := test_preds_avg]

# Clip predictions to reasonable range (optional, remove if it hurts)
# submission[, traffic_volume := pmax(0, pmin(traffic_volume, 7000))]

fwrite(submission, "submission_improved_v2.csv")
cat("Saved submission_improved_v2.csv\n")

saveRDS(models_list, file = "lgb_models_improved.rds")



# DIAGNOSTIC VERSION - Check for distribution mismatches

# After creating train_dt and test_dt, BEFORE training:

cat("\n=== DATA DISTRIBUTION CHECKS ===\n")

# Check feature distributions between train and test
cat("\nFeature Availability (% non-NA):\n")
for (f in feats) {
  train_na_pct <- sum(is.na(train_dt[[f]])) / nrow(train_dt) * 100
  test_na_pct <- sum(is.na(test_dt[[f]])) / nrow(test_dt) * 100
  cat(sprintf("%s: Train=%.1f%% NA, Test=%.1f%% NA\n", f, train_na_pct, test_na_pct))
}

# Check if test has very different ranges
cat("\nFeature Ranges (Train vs Test):\n")
numeric_feats <- feats[sapply(train_dt[, ..feats], is.numeric)]
for (f in numeric_feats[1:min(10, length(numeric_feats))]) {
  train_mean <- mean(train_dt[[f]], na.rm=TRUE)
  test_mean <- mean(test_dt[[f]], na.rm=TRUE)
  train_sd <- sd(train_dt[[f]], na.rm=TRUE)
  test_sd <- sd(test_dt[[f]], na.rm=TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("%s: Train mean=%.1f (sd=%.1f), Test mean=%.1f (sd=%.1f)\n", 
              f, train_mean, train_sd, test_mean, test_sd))
}

# Check temporal coverage
cat("\nTemporal Coverage:\n")
cat("Train date range:", as.character(min(train_dt$date_time)), "to", as.character(max(train_dt$date_time)), "\n")
cat("Test date range:", as.character(min(test_dt$date_time)), "to", as.character(max(test_dt$date_time)), "\n")

# Check if test is AFTER train (forward extrapolation = harder)
if (min(test_dt$date_time) > max(train_dt$date_time)) {
  cat("âš ď¸Ź  TEST IS FUTURE DATA - this is forward extrapolation (harder!)\n")
} else {
  cat("âś“ Test overlaps with train temporally\n")
}

cat("\n=== STRATEGY: Use conservative model to reduce overfitting ===\n")

# Use more conservative hyperparameters to reduce train/test gap
lgb_params <- list(
  objective = "regression",
  metric = "rmse",
  learning_rate = 0.01,         # Very conservative
  num_leaves = 31,              # Simpler model
  min_data_in_leaf = 50,        # Stronger constraint (was 10)
  feature_fraction = 0.6,       # More aggressive feature subsampling
  bagging_fraction = 0.6,       # More aggressive row subsampling
  bagging_freq = 5,
  lambda_l2 = 5.0,              # Strong L2 regularization
  lambda_l1 = 2.0,              # Strong L1 regularization
  max_depth = 8,                # Limit depth
  verbosity = -1
)

nrounds <- 5000
early_stopping_rounds <- 150   # More patient to find good solution

# IMPORTANT: Use stratified/time-aware split that respects temporal order
setorderv(train_dt, "date_time")

# Try LARGER initial window to get more stable training
n <- nrow(train_dt)
initial_prop <- 0.75          # Use more history (was 0.6)
assess_prop <- 0.1
initial <- floor(n * initial_prop)
assess  <- floor(n * assess_prop)

ro <- rolling_origin(train_dt, initial = initial, assess = assess, cumulative = TRUE)
max_folds <- min(nrow(ro), 6)
ro <- ro[seq_len(max_folds), ]

cat("Using", nrow(ro), "rolling CV folds.\n")

oof_preds <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(train_dt))
oof_actual <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(train_dt))
test_preds_list <- list()
models_list <- list()
fold_rmses <- c()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(ro))) {
  split <- ro$splits[[i]]
  cat("Training fold", i, "of", nrow(ro), "...\n")
  
  tr_idx <- analysis(split)
  va_idx <- assessment(split)
  
  # Check fold distributions
  cat("  Train size:", nrow(tr_idx), "| Val size:", nrow(va_idx), "\n")
  cat("  Train traffic mean:", round(mean(tr_idx$traffic_volume, na.rm=TRUE), 0),
      "| Val traffic mean:", round(mean(va_idx$traffic_volume, na.rm=TRUE), 0), "\n")
  
  dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(tr_idx[, ..feats]), label = tr_idx$traffic_volume)
  dval   <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), label = va_idx$traffic_volume)
  
  model <- lgb.train(params = lgb_params,
                     data = dtrain,
                     valids = list(valid = dval),
                     nrounds = nrounds,
                     early_stopping_rounds = early_stopping_rounds,
                     verbose = 0)
  
  models_list[[i]] <- model

  # Validation predictions
  pred_val <- predict(model, as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), num_iteration = model$best_iter)
  idx_in_train <- match(va_idx$row_id, train_dt$row_id)
  oof_preds[idx_in_train] <- pred_val
  oof_actual[idx_in_train] <- va_idx$traffic_volume
  
  # Compute fold RMSE
  fold_rmse <- sqrt(mean((pred_val - va_idx$traffic_volume)^2))
  fold_rmses <- c(fold_rmses, fold_rmse)
  cat("  Fold RMSE:", round(fold_rmse, 2), "| Best iter:", model$best_iter, "\n")

  # Test predictions
  pred_test_fold <- predict(model, as.matrix(test_dt[, ..feats]), num_iteration = model$best_iter)
  test_preds_list[[i]] <- pred_test_fold

  gc()
}

# Aggregate test predictions
test_preds_avg <- colMeans(do.call(rbind, test_preds_list))

# Compute OOF RMSE (only on predictions made)
oo <- which(!is.na(oof_preds))
oof_rmse <- sqrt(mean((oof_preds[oo] - oof_actual[oo])^2))

cat("\n=== RESULTS ===\n")
cat("OOF RMSE:", round(oof_rmse, 4), "\n")
cat("Fold RMSEs:", paste(round(fold_rmses, 2), collapse=", "), "\n")
cat("Mean fold RMSE:", round(mean(fold_rmses), 4), "\n")
cat("SD of fold RMSEs:", round(sd(fold_rmses), 4), "\n")

if (sd(fold_rmses) > mean(fold_rmses) * 0.3) {
  cat("âš ď¸Ź  High variability in fold RMSEs - model may be unstable\n")
}

# Feature importance
if (length(models_list) > 0) {
  imp <- lgb.importance(models_list[[1]])
  cat("\nTop 15 Features:\n")
  print(head(imp, 15))
  fwrite(as.data.table(imp), file = "feature_importance_diagnostic.csv")
}

# Prepare submission
submission <- copy(test_dt[, .(ID)])
submission[, traffic_volume := test_preds_avg]

fwrite(submission, "submission_diagnostic.csv")
cat("\nSubmission saved with predictions:\n")
cat("  Min:", round(min(submission$traffic_volume), 2),
    "| Max:", round(max(submission$traffic_volume), 2),
    "| Mean:", round(mean(submission$traffic_volume), 2), "\n")

saveRDS(models_list, file = "lgb_models_diagnostic.rds")


# SIMPLER, CLEANER APPROACH - Focus on FEATURES not regularization
# Your issue: model generalizes fine (OOF = Kaggle) but RMSE is just not good enough
# Solution: Better features, not more regularization

# Split back to train and test
train_dt <- TRAFFIC[!is.na(traffic_volume)]
test_dt  <- TRAFFIC[is.na(traffic_volume)]

setorderv(train_dt, "date_time")

# ============ BETTER FEATURE ENGINEERING ============

# SIMPLER, CLEANER APPROACH - Focus on FEATURES not regularization
# Your issue: model generalizes fine (OOF = Kaggle) but RMSE is just not good enough
# Solution: Better features, not more regularization
# SIMPLER, CLEANER APPROACH - Focus on FEATURES not regularization
# Your issue: model generalizes fine (OOF = Kaggle) but RMSE is just not good enough
# Solution: Better features, not more regularization

# Split back to train and test
train_dt <- TRAFFIC[!is.na(traffic_volume)]
test_dt  <- TRAFFIC[is.na(traffic_volume)]

setorderv(train_dt, "date_time")

# ============ BETTER FEATURE ENGINEERING ============

# 1. Target encoding: what's the avg traffic at each hour/day combo?
hour_wday_avg <- train_dt[, .(hour_wday_mean = mean(traffic_volume, na.rm=TRUE)), 
                           by = .(hour, wday)]

train_dt <- merge(train_dt, hour_wday_avg, by = c("hour", "wday"), all.x = TRUE)
test_dt <- merge(test_dt, hour_wday_avg, by = c("hour", "wday"), all.x = TRUE)

# 2. Seasonal patterns: average traffic by month
month_avg <- train_dt[, .(month_mean = mean(traffic_volume, na.rm=TRUE)), by = month]
train_dt <- merge(train_dt, month_avg, by = "month", all.x = TRUE)
test_dt <- merge(test_dt, month_avg, by = "month", all.x = TRUE)

# 3. Better lag features - SIMPLE approach using shift()
# Since data is time-ordered, we can use shift for training data
for (h in c(1, 7, 24, 168)) {
  newcol <- paste0("lag_", h, "h")
  train_dt[, (newcol) := shift(traffic_volume, n = h, type = "lag")]
}

# For test data - use approximate join with rolling join
lookup_table <- train_dt[, .(date_time, traffic_volume)]
setkey(lookup_table, date_time)

for (h in c(1, 7, 24, 168)) {
  newcol <- paste0("lag_", h, "h")
  
  # Create desired lookup times
  test_dt[, desired_time := date_time - dhours(h)]
  
  # Use rolling join (roll = "nearest")
  test_lookup <- copy(test_dt[, .(row_idx = .I, desired_time)])
  setkey(test_lookup, desired_time)
  
  result <- lookup_table[test_lookup, 
                         on = "date_time", 
                         roll = "nearest", 
                         .(traffic_volume)]
  
  test_dt[, (newcol) := result$traffic_volume]
  test_dt[, desired_time := NULL]
}

# 4. Simple but powerful: diff features
train_dt[, lag_1h_diff := lag_1h - lag_24h]  # change from 1h ago vs 24h ago
test_dt[, lag_1h_diff := lag_1h - lag_24h]

# 5. Ratio to typical: compare current-like to what's normal
train_dt[, to_hour_wday_mean := ifelse(is.na(hour_wday_mean), 1, lag_1h / (hour_wday_mean + 1))]
test_dt[, to_hour_wday_mean := ifelse(is.na(hour_wday_mean), 1, lag_1h / (hour_wday_mean + 1))]

# 6. Weather interactions (simple)
train_dt[, temp_rain := temp * rain_1h]
test_dt[, temp_rain := temp * rain_1h]

# ============ SIMPLE FEATURE LIST ============
base_feats <- c(
  # Time features
  "hour", "wday", "month",
  "sin_hour", "cos_hour", "sin_wk", "cos_wk",
  
  # Weather
  "temp", "rain_1h", "snow_1h", "clouds_all", "temp_rain",
  
  # Lags (most important for time series!)
  "lag_1h", "lag_7h", "lag_24h", "lag_168h",
  
  # Derived
  "lag_1h_diff", "to_hour_wday_mean",
  
  # Target encoding (seasonal patterns)
  "hour_wday_mean", "month_mean"
)

feats <- intersect(base_feats, names(train_dt))

# Replace Inf with NA
for (f in feats) {
  set(train_dt, which(is.infinite(train_dt[[f]])), f, NA)
  set(test_dt,  which(is.infinite(test_dt[[f]])), f, NA)
}

cat("Using", length(feats), "features:\n")
print(feats)

# ============ SIMPLE ROLLING CV ============
n <- nrow(train_dt)
initial <- floor(n * 0.7)
assess <- floor(n * 0.1)

ro <- rolling_origin(train_dt, initial = initial, assess = assess, cumulative = TRUE)
max_folds <- min(nrow(ro), 5)
ro <- ro[seq_len(max_folds), ]

cat("\nUsing", nrow(ro), "rolling CV folds\n")

# ============ LIGHTGBM - TUNED FOR SPEED AND SIMPLICITY ============
lgb_params <- list(
  objective = "regression",
  metric = "rmse",
  learning_rate = 0.05,
  num_leaves = 31,
  min_data_in_leaf = 20,
  feature_fraction = 0.8,
  bagging_fraction = 0.8,
  bagging_freq = 5,
  verbosity = -1
)

nrounds <- 2000
early_stopping_rounds <- 50

oof_preds <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(train_dt))
test_preds_sum <- rep(0, nrow(test_dt))
models_list <- list()
fold_rmses <- c()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(ro))) {
  split <- ro$splits[[i]]
  cat("Fold", i, "...")
  
  tr_idx <- analysis(split)
  va_idx <- assessment(split)
  
  dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(tr_idx[, ..feats]), label = tr_idx$traffic_volume)
  dval   <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), label = va_idx$traffic_volume)
  
  model <- lgb.train(params = lgb_params,
                     data = dtrain,
                     valids = list(valid = dval),
                     nrounds = nrounds,
                     early_stopping_rounds = early_stopping_rounds,
                     verbose = 0)
  
  models_list[[i]] <- model
  
  pred_val <- predict(model, as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), num_iteration = model$best_iter)
  idx_in_train <- match(va_idx$row_id, train_dt$row_id)
  oof_preds[idx_in_train] <- pred_val
  
  fold_rmse <- sqrt(mean((pred_val - va_idx$traffic_volume)^2))
  fold_rmses <- c(fold_rmses, fold_rmse)
  cat("RMSE =", round(fold_rmse, 2), "\n")
  
  pred_test_fold <- predict(model, as.matrix(test_dt[, ..feats]), num_iteration = model$best_iter)
  test_preds_sum <- test_preds_sum + pred_test_fold
  
  gc()
}

test_preds_avg <- test_preds_sum / length(models_list)

# RESULTS
oo <- which(!is.na(oof_preds))
oof_rmse <- sqrt(mean((oof_preds[oo] - train_dt$traffic_volume[oo])^2))

cat("\n=== RESULTS ===\n")
cat("OOF RMSE:", round(oof_rmse, 2), "\n")
cat("Fold RMSEs:", paste(round(fold_rmses, 2), collapse=", "), "\n")

# Feature importance
imp <- lgb.importance(models_list[[1]])
cat("\nTop 15 Features:\n")
print(head(imp, 15))

# SUBMISSION
submission <- copy(test_dt[, .(ID)])
submission[, traffic_volume := test_preds_avg]
fwrite(submission, "submission_simple_features.csv")
cat("\nSubmission saved!\n")

saveRDS(models_list, file = "lgb_models_simple.rds")
# Split back to train and test
train_dt <- TRAFFIC[!is.na(traffic_volume)]
test_dt  <- TRAFFIC[is.na(traffic_volume)]

setorderv(train_dt, "date_time")

# ============ BETTER FEATURE ENGINEERING ============

# 1. Target encoding: what's the avg traffic at each hour/day combo?
hour_wday_avg <- train_dt[, .(hour_wday_mean = mean(traffic_volume, na.rm=TRUE)), 
                           by = .(hour, wday)]

train_dt <- merge(train_dt, hour_wday_avg, by = c("hour", "wday"), all.x = TRUE)
test_dt <- merge(test_dt, hour_wday_avg, by = c("hour", "wday"), all.x = TRUE)

# 2. Seasonal patterns: average traffic by month
month_avg <- train_dt[, .(month_mean = mean(traffic_volume, na.rm=TRUE)), by = month]
train_dt <- merge(train_dt, month_avg, by = "month", all.x = TRUE)
test_dt <- merge(test_dt, month_avg, by = "month", all.x = TRUE)

# 3. Better lag features - add more lags (FIXED)
# Create lookup table from TRAIN data only
lookup_table <- train_dt[!is.na(traffic_volume), .(date_time, traffic_volume)]
setkey(lookup_table, date_time)

for (h in c(1, 7, 24, 168)) {  # 1h, 7h, 24h (same time yesterday), 168h (same time last week)
  newcol <- paste0("lag_", h, "h")
  
  # For TRAIN: find matching times h hours ago
  train_dt[, desired_time := date_time - dhours(h)]
  train_dt[, (newcol) := lookup_table[data.table(date_time = train_dt$desired_time), 
                                       on = "date_time", 
                                       x.traffic_volume]]
  train_dt[, desired_time := NULL]
  
  # For TEST: find matching times h hours ago (from train data)
  test_dt[, desired_time := date_time - dhours(h)]
  test_dt[, (newcol) := lookup_table[data.table(date_time = test_dt$desired_time), 
                                      on = "date_time", 
                                      x.traffic_volume]]
  test_dt[, desired_time := NULL]
}

# 4. Simple but powerful: diff features
train_dt[, lag_1h_diff := lag_1h - lag_24h]  # change from 1h ago vs 24h ago
test_dt[, lag_1h_diff := lag_1h - lag_24h]

# 5. Ratio to typical: compare current-like to what's normal
train_dt[, to_hour_wday_mean := ifelse(is.na(hour_wday_mean), 1, lag_1h / (hour_wday_mean + 1))]
test_dt[, to_hour_wday_mean := ifelse(is.na(hour_wday_mean), 1, lag_1h / (hour_wday_mean + 1))]

# 6. Weather interactions (simple)
train_dt[, temp_rain := temp * rain_1h]
test_dt[, temp_rain := temp * rain_1h]

# ============ SIMPLE FEATURE LIST ============
base_feats <- c(
  # Time features
  "hour", "wday", "month",
  "sin_hour", "cos_hour", "sin_wk", "cos_wk",
  
  # Weather
  "temp", "rain_1h", "snow_1h", "clouds_all", "temp_rain",
  
  # Lags (most important for time series!)
  "lag_1h", "lag_7h", "lag_24h", "lag_168h",
  
  # Derived
  "lag_1h_diff", "to_hour_wday_mean",
  
  # Target encoding (seasonal patterns)
  "hour_wday_mean", "month_mean"
)

feats <- intersect(base_feats, names(train_dt))

# Replace Inf with NA
for (f in feats) {
  set(train_dt, which(is.infinite(train_dt[[f]])), f, NA)
  set(test_dt,  which(is.infinite(test_dt[[f]])), f, NA)
}

cat("Using", length(feats), "features:\n")
print(feats)

# ============ SIMPLE ROLLING CV ============
n <- nrow(train_dt)
initial <- floor(n * 0.7)
assess <- floor(n * 0.1)

ro <- rolling_origin(train_dt, initial = initial, assess = assess, cumulative = TRUE)
max_folds <- min(nrow(ro), 5)
ro <- ro[seq_len(max_folds), ]

cat("\nUsing", nrow(ro), "rolling CV folds\n")

# ============ LIGHTGBM - TUNED FOR SPEED AND SIMPLICITY ============
lgb_params <- list(
  objective = "regression",
  metric = "rmse",
  learning_rate = 0.05,
  num_leaves = 31,
  min_data_in_leaf = 20,
  feature_fraction = 0.8,
  bagging_fraction = 0.8,
  bagging_freq = 5,
  verbosity = -1
)

nrounds <- 2000
early_stopping_rounds <- 50

oof_preds <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(train_dt))
test_preds_sum <- rep(0, nrow(test_dt))
models_list <- list()
fold_rmses <- c()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(ro))) {
  split <- ro$splits[[i]]
  cat("Fold", i, "...")
  
  tr_idx <- analysis(split)
  va_idx <- assessment(split)
  
  dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(tr_idx[, ..feats]), label = tr_idx$traffic_volume)
  dval   <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), label = va_idx$traffic_volume)
  
  model <- lgb.train(params = lgb_params,
                     data = dtrain,
                     valids = list(valid = dval),
                     nrounds = nrounds,
                     early_stopping_rounds = early_stopping_rounds,
                     verbose = 0)
  
  models_list[[i]] <- model
  
  pred_val <- predict(model, as.matrix(va_idx[, ..feats]), num_iteration = model$best_iter)
  idx_in_train <- match(va_idx$row_id, train_dt$row_id)
  oof_preds[idx_in_train] <- pred_val
  
  fold_rmse <- sqrt(mean((pred_val - va_idx$traffic_volume)^2))
  fold_rmses <- c(fold_rmses, fold_rmse)
  cat("RMSE =", round(fold_rmse, 2), "\n")
  
  pred_test_fold <- predict(model, as.matrix(test_dt[, ..feats]), num_iteration = model$best_iter)
  test_preds_sum <- test_preds_sum + pred_test_fold
  
  gc()
}

test_preds_avg <- test_preds_sum / length(models_list)

# RESULTS
oo <- which(!is.na(oof_preds))
oof_rmse <- sqrt(mean((oof_preds[oo] - train_dt$traffic_volume[oo])^2))

cat("\n=== RESULTS ===\n")
cat("OOF RMSE:", round(oof_rmse, 2), "\n")
cat("Fold RMSEs:", paste(round(fold_rmses, 2), collapse=", "), "\n")

# Feature importance
imp <- lgb.importance(models_list[[1]])
cat("\nTop 15 Features:\n")
print(head(imp, 15))

# SUBMISSION
submission <- copy(test_dt[, .(ID)])
submission[, traffic_volume := test_preds_avg]
fwrite(submission, "submission_simple_features.csv")
cat("\nSubmission saved!\n")

saveRDS(models_list, file = "lgb_models_simple.rds")

#DO data wrangling, feature engineering, etc., as you see fit
#Do NOT use the `date_time` column as-is since it's a factor with 9804 levels and models will CHOKE
#Plus since most values are unique, it won't do any good treating each value separately

TRAFFIC$snow_1h <- NULL

TRAFFIC$date_time <- lubridate::ymd_hms(as.character(TRAFFIC$date_time))



# Extract time-based features
TRAFFIC$hour <- lubridate::hour(TRAFFIC$date_time)
TRAFFIC$day_of_week <- lubridate::wday(TRAFFIC$date_time, label = TRUE)
TRAFFIC$month <- lubridate::month(TRAFFIC$date_time, label = TRUE)
TRAFFIC$day_of_month <- lubridate::day(TRAFFIC$date_time)
TRAFFIC$year <- lubridate::year(TRAFFIC$date_time)

# Weekend indicator
TRAFFIC$is_weekend <- factor(ifelse(lubridate::wday(TRAFFIC$date_time) %in% c(1, 7), "Yes", "No"))

# Time of day categories
TRAFFIC$time_of_day <- factor(case_when(
  TRAFFIC$hour >= 6 & TRAFFIC$hour < 10 ~ "Morning_Rush",
  TRAFFIC$hour >= 10 & TRAFFIC$hour < 16 ~ "Midday",
  TRAFFIC$hour >= 16 & TRAFFIC$hour < 19 ~ "Evening_Rush",
  TRAFFIC$hour >= 19 & TRAFFIC$hour < 23 ~ "Evening",
  TRUE ~ "Night"
))

# Rush hour indicator (7-9 AM and 4-6 PM)
TRAFFIC$rush_hour <- factor(ifelse(
  (TRAFFIC$hour >= 7 & TRAFFIC$hour <= 9) | (TRAFFIC$hour >= 16 & TRAFFIC$hour <= 18),
  "Yes", "No"
))

# Working hours (9 AM - 5 PM weekdays)
TRAFFIC$work_hours <- factor(ifelse(
  TRAFFIC$hour >= 9 & TRAFFIC$hour <= 17 & TRAFFIC$is_weekend == "No",
  "Yes", "No"
))

# Season
TRAFFIC$season <- factor(case_when(
  TRAFFIC$month %in% c("Dec", "Jan", "Feb") ~ "Winter",
  TRAFFIC$month %in% c("Mar", "Apr", "May") ~ "Spring",
  TRAFFIC$month %in% c("Jun", "Jul", "Aug") ~ "Summer",
  TRUE ~ "Fall"
))

# Verify extraction worked
cat("Feature extraction check:\n")
cat("Hour range:", range(TRAFFIC$hour, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Days of week:", levels(TRAFFIC$day_of_week), "\n")
cat("Months:", levels(TRAFFIC$month), "\n")
cat("Years:", unique(TRAFFIC$year), "\n")


# First, let's look at the format of date_time
cat("Sample date_time values from TRAIN:\n")
print(head(as.character(TRAIN$date_time), 10))
cat("\nSample date_time values from KAGGLE:\n")
print(head(as.character(KAGGLE$date_time), 10))

# Stack the datasets fresh
TRAFFIC <- rbind(TRAIN, KAGGLE)

# Remove snow_1h (all zeros)
TRAFFIC$snow_1h <- NULL

# Convert date_time with better error handling
TRAFFIC$date_time_char <- as.character(TRAFFIC$date_time)

# Try parsing with lubridate
TRAFFIC$date_time <- lubridate::ymd_hms(TRAFFIC$date_time_char, quiet = FALSE)

# Check how many failed
failed_count <- sum(is.na(TRAFFIC$date_time))
cat("\nFailed to parse:", failed_count, "out of", nrow(TRAFFIC), "rows\n")

# If there are failures, try alternative parsing
if(failed_count > 0) {
  cat("Attempting alternative parsing methods...\n")
  
  # Try base R as.POSIXct
  still_na <- is.na(TRAFFIC$date_time)
  TRAFFIC$date_time[still_na] <- as.POSIXct(
    TRAFFIC$date_time_char[still_na], 
    format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"
  )
  
  cat("After alternative parsing, failed:", sum(is.na(TRAFFIC$date_time)), "\n")
}

# Extract features (using lubridate with :: prefix)
TRAFFIC$hour <- lubridate::hour(TRAFFIC$date_time)
TRAFFIC$day_of_week <- lubridate::wday(TRAFFIC$date_time, label = TRUE)
TRAFFIC$month <- lubridate::month(TRAFFIC$date_time, label = TRUE)
TRAFFIC$day_of_month <- lubridate::day(TRAFFIC$date_time)
TRAFFIC$year <- lubridate::year(TRAFFIC$date_time)

# Verify
cat("\n=== VERIFICATION ===\n")
cat("Hour range:", range(TRAFFIC$hour, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Hour 0 count:", sum(TRAFFIC$hour == 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Years:", sort(unique(TRAFFIC$year)), "\n")
cat("NA count in year:", sum(is.na(TRAFFIC$year)), "\n")

# Clean up temporary column
TRAFFIC$date_time_char <- NULL

# Bad weather indicator (only rain since snow is all zeros)
TRAFFIC$bad_weather <- factor(ifelse(TRAFFIC$rain_1h > 0, "Yes", "No"))

# Has rain (simple binary)
TRAFFIC$has_rain <- factor(ifelse(TRAFFIC$rain_1h > 0, "Yes", "No"))

# Precipitation total (just rain)
TRAFFIC$precip_total <- TRAFFIC$rain_1h

# Weather severity categories (based on rain only)
TRAFFIC$weather_severity <- factor(case_when(
  TRAFFIC$rain_1h == 0 ~ "None",
  TRAFFIC$rain_1h > 0 & TRAFFIC$rain_1h <= 1 ~ "Light",
  TRAFFIC$rain_1h > 1 & TRAFFIC$rain_1h <= 3 ~ "Moderate",
  TRUE ~ "Heavy"
))

# Temperature categories (adjusted thresholds for better spread)
TRAFFIC$temp_category <- factor(case_when(
  TRAFFIC$temp < 10 ~ "Very_Cold",
  TRAFFIC$temp >= 10 & TRAFFIC$temp < 32 ~ "Cold",
  TRAFFIC$temp >= 32 & TRAFFIC$temp < 50 ~ "Cool",
  TRAFFIC$temp >= 50 & TRAFFIC$temp < 70 ~ "Mild",
  TRUE ~ "Warm"
))

# Cloud cover categories
TRAFFIC$cloud_category <- factor(case_when(
  TRAFFIC$clouds_all < 25 ~ "Clear",
  TRAFFIC$clouds_all >= 25 & TRAFFIC$clouds_all < 50 ~ "Partly_Cloudy",
  TRAFFIC$clouds_all >= 50 & TRAFFIC$clouds_all < 75 ~ "Mostly_Cloudy",
  TRUE ~ "Overcast"
))

# Simplify weather_main (keep top 5 categories, lump rest as "Other")
TRAFFIC$weather_simple <- fct_lump_n(TRAFFIC$weather_main, n = 5, other_level = "Other")

# Simplify weather_description (keep top 10, too many unique values otherwise)
TRAFFIC$weather_desc_simple <- fct_lump_n(TRAFFIC$weather_description, n = 10, other_level = "Other")

#==============================================================================
# HOLIDAY ENGINEERING
#==============================================================================

# Create binary holiday indicator
TRAFFIC$is_holiday <- factor(ifelse(TRAFFIC$holiday == "None", "No", "Yes"))

# Holiday type categories
TRAFFIC$holiday_type <- factor(case_when(
  TRAFFIC$holiday == "None" ~ "None",
  str_detect(as.character(TRAFFIC$holiday), "Christmas|Thanksgiving|New Year") ~ "Major",
  TRUE ~ "Minor"
))

cat("=== CHECKING EXISTING FEATURES ===\n")
cat("rush_hour exists:", "rush_hour" %in% names(TRAFFIC), "\n")
cat("has_rain exists:", "has_rain" %in% names(TRAFFIC), "\n")
cat("is_weekend exists:", "is_weekend" %in% names(TRAFFIC), "\n")
cat("time_of_day exists:", "time_of_day" %in% names(TRAFFIC), "\n")

if(!"is_weekend" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$is_weekend <- factor(ifelse(lubridate::wday(TRAFFIC$date_time) %in% c(1, 7), "Yes", "No"))
}

# Time of day categories (if not already created)
if(!"time_of_day" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$time_of_day <- factor(case_when(
    TRAFFIC$hour >= 6 & TRAFFIC$hour < 10 ~ "Morning_Rush",
    TRAFFIC$hour >= 10 & TRAFFIC$hour < 16 ~ "Midday",
    TRAFFIC$hour >= 16 & TRAFFIC$hour < 19 ~ "Evening_Rush",
    TRAFFIC$hour >= 19 & TRAFFIC$hour < 23 ~ "Evening",
    TRUE ~ "Night"
  ))
}

if(!"rush_hour" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$rush_hour <- factor(ifelse(
    (TRAFFIC$hour >= 7 & TRAFFIC$hour <= 9) | (TRAFFIC$hour >= 16 & TRAFFIC$hour <= 18),
    "Yes", "No"
  ))
}

# Working hours (if not already created)
if(!"work_hours" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$work_hours <- factor(ifelse(
    TRAFFIC$hour >= 9 & TRAFFIC$hour <= 17 & TRAFFIC$is_weekend == "No",
    "Yes", "No"
  ))
}


# Season (if not already created)
if(!"season" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$season <- factor(case_when(
    TRAFFIC$month %in% c("Dec", "Jan", "Feb") ~ "Winter",
    TRAFFIC$month %in% c("Mar", "Apr", "May") ~ "Spring",
    TRAFFIC$month %in% c("Jun", "Jul", "Aug") ~ "Summer",
    TRUE ~ "Fall"
  ))
}

# Bad weather indicator
if(!"bad_weather" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$bad_weather <- factor(ifelse(TRAFFIC$rain_1h > 0, "Yes", "No"))
}

# Has rain
if(!"has_rain" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$has_rain <- factor(ifelse(TRAFFIC$rain_1h > 0, "Yes", "No"))
}

# Precipitation total
if(!"precip_total" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$precip_total <- TRAFFIC$rain_1h
}

# Weather severity categories
if(!"weather_severity" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$weather_severity <- factor(case_when(
    TRAFFIC$rain_1h == 0 ~ "None",
    TRAFFIC$rain_1h > 0 & TRAFFIC$rain_1h <= 1 ~ "Light",
    TRAFFIC$rain_1h > 1 & TRAFFIC$rain_1h <= 3 ~ "Moderate",
    TRUE ~ "Heavy"
  ))
}

# Temperature categories
if(!"temp_category" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$temp_category <- factor(case_when(
    TRAFFIC$temp < 10 ~ "Very_Cold",
    TRAFFIC$temp >= 10 & TRAFFIC$temp < 32 ~ "Cold",
    TRAFFIC$temp >= 32 & TRAFFIC$temp < 50 ~ "Cool",
    TRAFFIC$temp >= 50 & TRAFFIC$temp < 70 ~ "Mild",
    TRUE ~ "Warm"
  ))
}

# Cloud cover categories
if(!"cloud_category" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$cloud_category <- factor(case_when(
    TRAFFIC$clouds_all < 25 ~ "Clear",
    TRAFFIC$clouds_all >= 25 & TRAFFIC$clouds_all < 50 ~ "Partly_Cloudy",
    TRAFFIC$clouds_all >= 50 & TRAFFIC$clouds_all < 75 ~ "Mostly_Cloudy",
    TRUE ~ "Overcast"
  ))
}

# Simplify weather_main
if(!"weather_simple" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$weather_simple <- fct_lump_n(TRAFFIC$weather_main, n = 5, other_level = "Other")
}

# Simplify weather_description
if(!"weather_desc_simple" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$weather_desc_simple <- fct_lump_n(TRAFFIC$weather_description, n = 10, other_level = "Other")
}

#==============================================================================
# HOLIDAY FEATURES
#==============================================================================

if(!"is_holiday" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$is_holiday <- factor(ifelse(TRAFFIC$holiday == "None", "No", "Yes"))
}

if(!"holiday_type" %in% names(TRAFFIC)) {
  TRAFFIC$holiday_type <- factor(case_when(
    TRAFFIC$holiday == "None" ~ "None",
    str_detect(as.character(TRAFFIC$holiday), "Christmas|Thanksgiving|New Year") ~ "Major",
    TRUE ~ "Minor"
  ))
}

cat("\n=== CREATING INTERACTION FEATURES ===\n")

# Verify prerequisites exist
cat("rush_hour exists:", "rush_hour" %in% names(TRAFFIC), "\n")
cat("has_rain exists:", "has_rain" %in% names(TRAFFIC), "\n")
cat("is_weekend exists:", "is_weekend" %in% names(TRAFFIC), "\n")
cat("time_of_day exists:", "time_of_day" %in% names(TRAFFIC), "\n")

# Rush hour + rain
TRAFFIC$rush_rain <- interaction(TRAFFIC$rush_hour, TRAFFIC$has_rain, drop = TRUE)

# Weekend + time of day
TRAFFIC$weekend_time <- interaction(TRAFFIC$is_weekend, TRAFFIC$time_of_day, drop = TRUE)

# Temperature x rain
TRAFFIC$temp_rain <- TRAFFIC$temp * TRAFFIC$rain_1h

# Hour x day of week
TRAFFIC$hour_dow <- interaction(TRAFFIC$hour, TRAFFIC$day_of_week, drop = TRUE)

# Day of week x weather
TRAFFIC$dow_weather <- interaction(TRAFFIC$day_of_week, TRAFFIC$weather_simple, drop = TRUE)

cat("Interaction features created successfully!\n")

#==============================================================================
# CYCLIC FEATURES
#==============================================================================

TRAFFIC$hour_sin <- sin(2 * pi * TRAFFIC$hour / 24)
TRAFFIC$hour_cos <- cos(2 * pi * TRAFFIC$hour / 24)
TRAFFIC$dow_sin <- sin(2 * pi * as.numeric(TRAFFIC$day_of_week) / 7)
TRAFFIC$dow_cos <- cos(2 * pi * as.numeric(TRAFFIC$day_of_week) / 7)
TRAFFIC$month_sin <- sin(2 * pi * as.numeric(TRAFFIC$month) / 12)
TRAFFIC$month_cos <- cos(2 * pi * as.numeric(TRAFFIC$month) / 12)

#==============================================================================
# ADDITIONAL FEATURES
#==============================================================================

TRAFFIC$temp_squared <- TRAFFIC$temp^2

TRAFFIC$commute_hour <- factor(ifelse(
  TRAFFIC$hour %in% c(7, 8, 9, 16, 17, 18), "Yes", "No"
))

TRAFFIC$late_night <- factor(ifelse(
  TRAFFIC$hour >= 23 | TRAFFIC$hour <= 5, "Yes", "No"
))

#==============================================================================
# CLEAN UP
#==============================================================================

TRAFFIC_CLEAN <- TRAFFIC %>%
  dplyr::select(-date_time, -ID, -weather_main, -weather_description, -holiday)

cat("\n=== FEATURE ENGINEERING COMPLETE ===\n")
cat("Total features:", ncol(TRAFFIC_CLEAN), "\n")

# Split
TRAIN.CLEAN <- TRAFFIC_CLEAN[1:nrow(TRAIN), ]
KAGGLE.CLEAN <- TRAFFIC_CLEAN[(nrow(TRAIN) + 1):nrow(TRAFFIC_CLEAN), ]
KAGGLE.CLEAN$traffic_volume <- NULL

cat("TRAIN.CLEAN rows:", nrow(TRAIN.CLEAN), "\n")
cat("KAGGLE.CLEAN rows:", nrow(KAGGLE.CLEAN), "\n")
cat("TRAIN.CLEAN features:", ncol(TRAIN.CLEAN), "\n")
cat("KAGGLE.CLEAN features:", ncol(KAGGLE.CLEAN), "\n")

glimpse(TRAIN.CLEAN)

#==============================================================================
# CYCLIC FEATURES (for neural networks and some models)
#==============================================================================

# Hour as sine/cosine (captures cyclical nature of 24-hour day)
TRAFFIC$hour_sin <- sin(2 * pi * TRAFFIC$hour / 24)
TRAFFIC$hour_cos <- cos(2 * pi * TRAFFIC$hour / 24)

# Day of week as sine/cosine (captures weekly cycle)
TRAFFIC$dow_sin <- sin(2 * pi * as.numeric(TRAFFIC$day_of_week) / 7)
TRAFFIC$dow_cos <- cos(2 * pi * as.numeric(TRAFFIC$day_of_week) / 7)

# Month as sine/cosine (captures annual seasonality)
TRAFFIC$month_sin <- sin(2 * pi * as.numeric(TRAFFIC$month) / 12)
TRAFFIC$month_cos <- cos(2 * pi * as.numeric(TRAFFIC$month) / 12)

#==============================================================================
# ADDITIONAL USEFUL FEATURES
#==============================================================================

# Temperature squared (for non-linear relationships)
TRAFFIC$temp_squared <- TRAFFIC$temp^2

# Is it a typical commute hour?
TRAFFIC$commute_hour <- factor(ifelse(
  TRAFFIC$hour %in% c(7, 8, 9, 16, 17, 18), "Yes", "No"
))

# Late night indicator
TRAFFIC$late_night <- factor(ifelse(TRAFFIC$hour >= 23 | TRAFFIC$hour <= 5, "Yes", "No"))

#==============================================================================
# CLEAN UP AND PREPARE FOR MODELING
#==============================================================================

# Create cleaned dataset (remove original date_time, ID, and redundant columns)
TRAFFIC_CLEAN <- TRAFFIC %>%
  dplyr::select(-date_time, -ID, -weather_main, -weather_description, -holiday)

# Check structure
cat("\n=== TRAFFIC_CLEAN STRUCTURE ===\n")
glimpse(TRAFFIC_CLEAN)

# Check for any remaining NAs (besides traffic_volume in KAGGLE portion)
cat("\n=== NA CHECK ===\n")
na_counts <- colSums(is.na(TRAFFIC_CLEAN))
na_counts_nonzero <- na_counts[na_counts > 0]
if(length(na_counts_nonzero) > 0) {
  print(na_counts_nonzero)
} else {
  cat("No NAs found (except traffic_volume in KAGGLE portion)!\n")
}

#==============================================================================
# SPLIT BACK INTO TRAIN AND KAGGLE
#==============================================================================

TRAIN.CLEAN <- TRAFFIC_CLEAN[1:nrow(TRAIN), ]
KAGGLE.CLEAN <- TRAFFIC_CLEAN[(nrow(TRAIN) + 1):nrow(TRAFFIC_CLEAN), ]

# Remove traffic_volume from KAGGLE.CLEAN (it's all NA)
KAGGLE.CLEAN$traffic_volume <- NULL

#==============================================================================
# FINAL VERIFICATION
#==============================================================================

cat("\n=== FINAL VERIFICATION ===\n")
cat("TRAIN.CLEAN rows:", nrow(TRAIN.CLEAN), "(should be 5000)\n")
cat("KAGGLE.CLEAN rows:", nrow(KAGGLE.CLEAN), "(should be 6572)\n")
cat("TRAIN has traffic_volume:", !all(is.na(TRAIN.CLEAN$traffic_volume)), "\n")
cat("KAGGLE has traffic_volume:", "traffic_volume" %in% names(KAGGLE.CLEAN), "(should be FALSE)\n")
cat("\n")

# Check feature counts
cat("Total features in TRAIN.CLEAN:", ncol(TRAIN.CLEAN), "\n")
cat("Total features in KAGGLE.CLEAN:", ncol(KAGGLE.CLEAN), "\n")
cat("\n")

# Check for zero-variance predictors
cat("Checking for zero-variance predictors...\n")
zero_var <- sapply(TRAIN.CLEAN, function(x) {
  if(is.numeric(x)) {
    var(x, na.rm = TRUE) == 0
  } else if(is.factor(x)) {
    length(unique(x)) <= 1
  } else {
    FALSE
  }
})

if(any(zero_var)) {
  cat("Zero-variance predictors found:\n")
  print(names(TRAIN.CLEAN)[zero_var])
} else {
  cat("No zero-variance predictors found! âś“\n")
}
cat("\n")

# Check factor levels (make sure none are too numerous)
cat("Factor variables and their levels:\n")
factor_levels <- sapply(TRAIN.CLEAN, function(x) {
  if(is.factor(x)) nlevels(x) else NA
})
factor_levels <- factor_levels[!is.na(factor_levels)]
print(sort(factor_levels, decreasing = TRUE))

cat("\n=== SUMMARY STATISTICS ===\n")
summary(TRAIN.CLEAN$traffic_volume)

cat("\n=== Feature Engineering Complete! ===\n")
cat("Ready for modeling!\n")




#After "cleaning", re-make the training and holdout samples.  You'll fit your models on TRAIN.CLEAN
#and make predictions on KAGGLE.CLEAN

TRAIN.CLEAN <- TRAFFIC[1:nrow(TRAIN),]
TRAIN.CLEAN$ID <- NULL  #Don't use this as a predictor, it's just a row label
TRAIN.CLEAN$date_time <- NULL  #Again, you don't want to use this as-is
KAGGLE.CLEAN <- TRAFFIC[-(1:nrow(TRAIN)),]



#NOTE:  your TRAIN.CLEAN should *NOT* have the ID or date_time columns in it!!!!
summary(TRAIN.CLEAN)
summary(KAGGLE.CLEAN)

#Model fitting time!

###############################################################################
# BZAN 542 - Traffic Volume Prediction Model Zoo
# Adapted for RapidRoute Logistics Hackathon
###############################################################################

library(regclass)
library(tidymodels)
library(tidyverse)
library(forcats)
library(lubridate)
library(kknn)
library(klaR)
library(discrim)
library(yardstick)
library(ggplot2)
library(doParallel)
library(vip)
library(DALEX)
library(DALEXtra)
library(pdp)
library(timeDate)
library(xgboost)
library(stacks)
library(h2o)

RNGkind(kind = "Mersenne-Twister", normal.kind = "Inversion", sample.kind = "Rejection")

# Setup parallel processing
cores <- parallel::detectCores()
cl <- makePSOCKcluster(cores - 1)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Create 5-fold CV
set.seed(542)
folds <- vfold_cv(TRAIN.CLEAN, v = 5)

# Initialize RMSE tracking dataframe
RMSE <- data.frame()

###############################################################################
# PART 1: LINEAR REGRESSION TYPE MODELS & KNN
###############################################################################

cat("\n=== STARTING LINEAR MODELS ===\n")

# Model engines
linreg <- linear_reg() %>% set_engine("lm")
elastic <- linear_reg(penalty = tune(), mixture = tune()) %>% set_engine("glmnet")
elastic_grid <- grid_regular(penalty(range = c(-4, -2)), mixture(range = c(0.5, 1)), levels = 4)
knn <- nearest_neighbor(neighbors = tune(), mode = "regression") %>% set_engine("kknn")
knn_grid <- grid_regular(neighbors(range = c(5, 50)), levels = 6)

# Naive model (baseline)
naive_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ 1, data = TRAIN.CLEAN)
naive_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(naive_recipe) %>% add_model(linreg)
naive_results <- fit_resamples(naive_workflow, resamples = folds)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(naive_results, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "naive"
cat("Naive model complete\n")

# Recipe 1: Basic (no interactions, no polynomials)
traffic_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors())

# Recipe 2: With polynomials
traffic_recipe_poly <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_poly(temp, temp_squared, degree = 2) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors())

# Recipe 3: With interactions
traffic_recipe_int <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ (temp + rain_1h + clouds_all + hour):starts_with("day_of_week_")) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ (temp + rain_1h + clouds_all + hour):starts_with("is_weekend_")) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ (temp + rain_1h + clouds_all + hour):starts_with("time_of_day_")) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ (temp + rain_1h + clouds_all + hour):starts_with("is_holiday_")) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ (temp + rain_1h + clouds_all + hour)^2) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors())

# Recipe 4: With interactions AND polynomials
traffic_recipe_int_poly <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ (temp + rain_1h + clouds_all + hour):starts_with("day_of_week_")) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ (temp + rain_1h + clouds_all + hour):starts_with("is_weekend_")) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ (temp + rain_1h + clouds_all + hour):starts_with("time_of_day_")) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ (temp + rain_1h + clouds_all + hour):starts_with("is_holiday_")) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ (temp + rain_1h + clouds_all + hour)^2) %>%
  step_poly(temp, temp_squared, degree = 2, keep_original_cols = TRUE) %>%  # Only temp variables
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors())

# Linear Regression - No interactions, No polynomials
linreg_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe) %>% add_model(linreg)
linreg_results <- fit_resamples(linreg_workflow, resamples = folds)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(linreg_results, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "linear"
cat("Linear model complete\n")

# Linear Regression - With polynomials
linreg_workflow_poly <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe_poly) %>% add_model(linreg)
linreg_results_poly <- fit_resamples(linreg_workflow_poly, resamples = folds)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(linreg_results_poly, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "linear+poly"
cat("Linear+poly model complete\n")

# Linear Regression - With interactions
linreg_workflow_int <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe_int) %>% add_model(linreg)
linreg_results_int <- fit_resamples(linreg_workflow_int, resamples = folds)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(linreg_results_int, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "linear+int"
cat("Linear+int model complete\n")

# Linear Regression - With interactions AND polynomials
linreg_workflow_int_poly <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe_int_poly) %>% add_model(linreg)
linreg_results_int_poly <- fit_resamples(linreg_workflow_int_poly, resamples = folds)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(linreg_results_int_poly, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "linear+int+poly"
cat("Linear+int+poly model complete\n")

# Elastic Net - No interactions, No polynomials
elastic_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe) %>% add_model(elastic)
elastic_results <- tune_grid(elastic_workflow, resamples = folds, grid = elastic_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(elastic_results, n = 1, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "elastic"
cat("Elastic model complete\n")

# Elastic Net - With polynomials
elastic_workflow_poly <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe_poly) %>% add_model(elastic)
elastic_results_poly <- tune_grid(elastic_workflow_poly, resamples = folds, grid = elastic_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(elastic_results_poly, n = 1, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "elastic+poly"
cat("Elastic+poly model complete\n")


# Elastic Net - With interactions
elastic_workflow_int <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe_int) %>% add_model(elastic)
elastic_results_int <- tune_grid(elastic_workflow_int, resamples = folds, grid = elastic_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(elastic_results_int, n = 1, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "elastic+int"
cat("Elastic+int model complete\n")

# Elastic Net - With interactions AND polynomials
elastic_workflow_int_poly <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe_int_poly) %>% add_model(elastic)
elastic_results_int_poly <- tune_grid(elastic_workflow_int_poly, resamples = folds, grid = elastic_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(elastic_results_int_poly, n = 1, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "elastic+int+poly"
cat("Elastic+int+poly model complete\n")

# KNN - No interactions, No polynomials
knn_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe) %>% add_model(knn)
knn_results <- tune_grid(knn_workflow, resamples = folds, grid = knn_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(knn_results, n = 1, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "knn"
cat("KNN model complete\n")

# KNN - With polynomials
knn_workflow_poly <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe_poly) %>% add_model(knn)
knn_results_poly <- tune_grid(knn_workflow_poly, resamples = folds, grid = knn_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(knn_results_poly, n = 1, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "knn+poly"
cat("KNN+poly model complete\n")

# KNN - With interactions
knn_workflow_int <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe_int) %>% add_model(knn)
knn_results_int <- tune_grid(knn_workflow_int, resamples = folds, grid = knn_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(knn_results_int, n = 1, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "knn+int"
cat("KNN+int model complete\n")

# KNN - With interactions AND polynomials
knn_workflow_int_poly <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(traffic_recipe_int_poly) %>% add_model(knn)
knn_results_int_poly <- tune_grid(knn_workflow_int_poly, resamples = folds, grid = knn_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(knn_results_int_poly, n = 1, metric = "rmse"))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "knn+int+poly"
cat("KNN+int+poly model complete\n")

# Display results so far
cat("\n=== LINEAR MODELS COMPLETE ===\n")
print(RMSE[, c("mean", "std_err", "name")] %>% arrange(mean))

###############################################################################
# PART 2: TREE-BASED MODELS
###############################################################################

cat("\n=== STARTING TREE MODELS ===\n")

# Vanilla Decision Tree
tree_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN)
tree_grid <- grid_regular(
  cost_complexity(range = c(-6, -3)),
  tree_depth(range = c(10, 20)),
  min_n(range = c(5, 20)),
  levels = 4
)
tree_model <- decision_tree(cost_complexity = tune(), tree_depth = tune(), min_n = tune()) %>%
  set_engine("rpart") %>% set_mode("regression")
tree_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(tree_recipe) %>% add_model(tree_model)
set.seed(542)
tree_results <- tune_grid(tree_workflow, resamples = folds, grid = tree_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(tree_results, metric = "rmse", n = 1))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "tree"
cat("Decision tree complete\n")

# Random Forest
forest_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN)
forest_model <- rand_forest(mtry = tune(), min_n = tune(), trees = 1000) %>%
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "impurity") %>% set_mode("regression")
forest_grid <- expand.grid(mtry = c(8, 12, 15, 20), min_n = c(2, 5, 10))
forest_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(forest_recipe) %>% add_model(forest_model)
set.seed(542)
forest_results <- tune_grid(forest_workflow, resamples = folds, grid = forest_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(forest_results, metric = "rmse", n = 1))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "forest"
cat("Random forest complete\n")

# Boosted Tree (basic)
boosted_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors())
boosted_model <- boost_tree(trees = tune(), tree_depth = tune(), learn_rate = tune(), min_n = tune()) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost") %>% set_mode("regression")
boosted_grid <- expand.grid(
  trees = c(500, 800, 1200),
  tree_depth = c(8, 12, 15),
  learn_rate = c(0.01, 0.02),
  min_n = c(5, 10)
)
boosted_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(boosted_recipe) %>% add_model(boosted_model)
set.seed(542)
boosted_results <- tune_grid(boosted_workflow, resamples = folds, grid = boosted_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(boosted_results, metric = "rmse", n = 1))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "boosted"
cat("Boosted tree complete\n")

# XGBoost (advanced tuning)
# XGBoost (FASTER - reduced grid)
xgboost_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors())

xgboost_model <- boost_tree(
  trees = tune(), tree_depth = tune(), learn_rate = tune(),
  min_n = tune(), loss_reduction = tune(), sample_size = tune(), mtry = tune()
) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost") %>% set_mode("regression")

# Reduced grid: 16 combinations instead of 648
set.seed(542)
xgboost_grid <- expand.grid(
  tree_depth = c(10, 15),
  min_n = c(5, 10),
  mtry = c(15, 20),
  trees = c(800, 1200),
  learn_rate = c(0.01),
  loss_reduction = c(0.00001),
  sample_size = c(0.7)
)

xgboost_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(xgboost_recipe) %>% add_model(xgboost_model)
xgboost_results <- tune_grid(xgboost_workflow, resamples = folds, grid = xgboost_grid)
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(xgboost_results, metric = "rmse", n = 1))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "xgboost"
cat("XGBoost complete\n")

# Fine-tune around your best boosted model parameters
boosted_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors())

boosted_model <- boost_tree(
  trees = tune(), 
  tree_depth = tune(), 
  learn_rate = tune(), 
  min_n = tune()
) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost") %>% 
  set_mode("regression")

# Focused grid around what worked
boosted_grid_fine <- expand.grid(
  trees = c(800, 1200, 1500),
  tree_depth = c(10, 12, 15),
  learn_rate = c(0.008, 0.01, 0.012),
  min_n = c(3, 5, 7)
)

boosted_workflow <- workflow() %>% 
  add_recipe(boosted_recipe) %>% 
  add_model(boosted_model)

set.seed(542)
boosted_results_fine <- tune_grid(
  boosted_workflow, 
  resamples = folds, 
  grid = boosted_grid_fine
)

# Check all results, not just best
show_best(boosted_results_fine, metric = "rmse", n = 10)

# Display results so far
cat("\n=== TREE MODELS COMPLETE ===\n")
result <- RMSE %>% arrange(mean)
print(result[, c("mean", "std_err", "name")])


library(h2o)

# Close everything first!
# Then initialize H2O with memory limits
h2o.init(
  ip = "127.0.0.1", 
  port = 54321, 
  nthreads = 4,        # Don't use all cores
  max_mem_size = "4G"  # Limit H2O to 4GB
)

# Prepare data
TRAIN.FOR.H2O <- TRAIN.CLEAN %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.ordered), ~ factor(., ordered = FALSE)))

KAGGLE.FOR.H2O <- KAGGLE.CLEAN %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.ordered), ~ factor(., ordered = FALSE)))

train_h2o <- as.h2o(TRAIN.FOR.H2O)
kaggle_h2o <- as.h2o(KAGGLE.FOR.H2O)

y <- "traffic_volume"
x <- setdiff(names(TRAIN.FOR.H2O), y)

# LIGHTWEIGHT AutoML
set.seed(542)
automl_models <- h2o.automl(
  x = x,
  y = y,
  training_frame = train_h2o,
  nfolds = 5,
  max_runtime_secs = 300,      # Only 5 minutes
  max_models = 15,              # Limit models
  seed = 542,
  exclude_algos = c("DeepLearning"),  # Skip deep learning (memory intensive)
  stopping_metric = "RMSE"
)

# Check leaderboard
print(automl_models@leaderboard)

# Get predictions
kaggle_predictions <- as.data.frame(h2o.predict(automl_models@leader, kaggle_h2o))$predict

# Save submission
submission <- data.frame(
  ID = KAGGLE$ID,
  traffic_volume = kaggle_predictions
)
write.csv(submission, "submission_h2o_lite.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# IMPORTANT: Shutdown
h2o.shutdown(prompt = FALSE)


# Shutdown current H2O
h2o.shutdown(prompt = FALSE)

# Restart with more time and models
h2o.init(max_mem_size = "4G", nthreads = 4)

# Same data prep...
train_h2o <- as.h2o(TRAIN.FOR.H2O)
kaggle_h2o <- as.h2o(KAGGLE.FOR.H2O)

# LONGER run (15 minutes, more models)
set.seed(542)
automl_models_v2 <- h2o.automl(
  x = x,
  y = y,
  training_frame = train_h2o,
  nfolds = 5,
  max_runtime_secs = 900,      # 15 minutes instead of 5
  max_models = 30,              # More models
  seed = 542,
  stopping_metric = "RMSE"
)

print(automl_models_v2@leaderboard)



# Shutdown H2O first
h2o.shutdown(prompt = FALSE)

# XGBoost recipe
xgboost_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors())

# Full XGBoost model
xgboost_model <- boost_tree(
  trees = tune(), 
  tree_depth = tune(), 
  learn_rate = tune(),
  min_n = tune(), 
  loss_reduction = tune(), 
  sample_size = tune(), 
  mtry = tune()
) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost") %>% 
  set_mode("regression")

# STRATEGY 1: Try more trees with slower learning (often wins competitions)
xgboost_grid_strategy1 <- expand.grid(
  trees = c(2000, 3000),           # More trees
  tree_depth = c(10, 12, 15),
  learn_rate = c(0.005, 0.008),    # Slower learning
  min_n = c(3, 5),
  loss_reduction = c(0, 0.0001),
  sample_size = c(0.8),            # Fixed
  mtry = c(20)                     # Fixed
)

xgboost_workflow <- workflow() %>% 
  add_recipe(xgboost_recipe) %>% 
  add_model(xgboost_model)

cat("Running XGBoost Strategy 1: More trees, slower learning...\n")
cat("Grid size:", nrow(xgboost_grid_strategy1), "combinations\n")
cat("Estimated time: 45-60 minutes\n")

set.seed(542)
xgboost_results_s1 <- tune_grid(
  xgboost_workflow, 
  resamples = folds, 
  grid = xgboost_grid_strategy1,
  control = control_grid(verbose = TRUE)
)

cat("\n=== STRATEGY 1 RESULTS ===\n")
show_best(xgboost_results_s1, metric = "rmse", n = 10)
best_s1 <- show_best(xgboost_results_s1, metric = "rmse", n = 1)
cat("\nBest RMSE:", best_s1$mean, "\n")

# Add to RMSE tracker
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, best_s1)
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "xgboost_s1"


# FINAL ATTEMPT: Ultra-focused around best parameters
xgboost_grid_final <- expand.grid(
  trees = c(3500, 4000, 4500),     # Even more trees
  tree_depth = c(15, 18, 20),      # Deeper
  learn_rate = c(0.007, 0.008, 0.009), # Fine-tune around 0.008
  min_n = c(3, 5, 7),              # Try different regularization
  loss_reduction = c(0.0001, 0.0005),
  sample_size = c(0.75, 0.8, 0.85), # Fine-tune subsampling
  mtry = c(18, 20, 22)              # Fine-tune feature sampling
)

cat("FINAL PUSH: Ultra-focused grid\n")
cat("Grid size:", nrow(xgboost_grid_final), "combinations\n")
cat("This will take 90-120 minutes\n")
cat("Best shot at breaking under 310!\n\n")

set.seed(542)
xgboost_results_final <- tune_grid(
  xgboost_workflow, 
  resamples = folds, 
  grid = xgboost_grid_final,
  control = control_grid(verbose = TRUE, save_pred = TRUE)
)

cat("\n=== FINAL RESULTS ===\n")
show_best(xgboost_results_final, metric = "rmse", n = 15)
best_final <- show_best(xgboost_results_final, metric = "rmse", n = 1)
cat("\nFINAL BEST RMSE:", best_final$mean, "\n")


# STOP the current run first!

# Your best boosted parameters were around:
# trees = 500-800, tree_depth = 8-15, learn_rate = 0.01, min_n = 5

# Let's fine-tune VERY specifically around what worked
boosted_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors())

boosted_model <- boost_tree(
  trees = tune(), 
  tree_depth = tune(), 
  learn_rate = tune(), 
  min_n = tune()
) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost") %>%
  set_mode("regression")

# SMART GRID: Fine-tune around your winner
# Only 72 combinations (vs 486!)
boosted_grid_final <- expand.grid(
  trees = c(600, 800, 1000, 1200),        # Around 800
  tree_depth = c(10, 12, 14),             # Around 12
  learn_rate = c(0.008, 0.01, 0.012),     # Around 0.01
  min_n = c(4, 5, 6)                      # Around 5
)

boosted_workflow <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(boosted_recipe) %>%
  add_model(boosted_model)

cat("FINAL BOOSTED OPTIMIZATION\n")
cat("Grid size:", nrow(boosted_grid_final), "models\n")
cat("Estimated time: 60-90 minutes\n\n")

set.seed(542)
boosted_results_final <- tune_grid(
  boosted_workflow,
  resamples = folds,
  grid = boosted_grid_final,
  control = control_grid(verbose = TRUE)
)

cat("\n=== FINAL BOOSTED RESULTS ===\n")
show_best(boosted_results_final, metric = "rmse", n = 10)

###############################################################################
# PART 3: SUPPORT VECTOR MACHINES
###############################################################################

cat("\n=== STARTING SVM MODELS ===\n")

svm_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors())

# SVM Linear
svm_linear_model <- svm_linear(mode = "regression", cost = tune()) %>% set_engine("kernlab")
svm_workflow_linear <- workflow() %>% add_model(svm_linear_model) %>% add_recipe(svm_recipe)
grid_linear <- grid_regular(cost(range = c(-2, 3)), levels = 4)
set.seed(542)
svm_results_linear <- tune_grid(svm_workflow_linear, resamples = folds, grid = grid_linear, metrics = metric_set(rmse))
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(svm_results_linear, metric = "rmse", n = 1))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "svm-linear"
cat("SVM linear complete\n")

# SVM Polynomial
svm_poly_model <- svm_poly(mode = "regression", cost = tune(), degree = tune(), scale_factor = tune()) %>%
  set_engine("kernlab")
grid_poly <- grid_regular(
  cost(range = c(-2, 2)),
  degree(range = c(2, 4)),
  scale_factor(range = c(-2, -1)),
  levels = 3
)
svm_workflow_poly <- workflow() %>% add_model(svm_poly_model) %>% add_recipe(svm_recipe)
set.seed(542)
svm_results_poly <- tune_grid(svm_workflow_poly, resamples = folds, grid = grid_poly, metrics = metric_set(rmse))
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(svm_results_poly, metric = "rmse", n = 1))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "svm-poly"
cat("SVM polynomial complete\n")

# SVM RBF
svm_rbf_model <- svm_rbf(mode = "regression", cost = tune(), rbf_sigma = tune()) %>%
  set_engine("kernlab")
grid_rbf <- grid_regular(cost(range = c(2, 6)), rbf_sigma(range = c(-3, 1)), levels = 4)
svm_workflow_rbf <- workflow() %>% add_model(svm_rbf_model) %>% add_recipe(svm_recipe)
set.seed(542)
svm_results_rbf <- tune_grid(svm_workflow_rbf, resamples = folds, grid = grid_rbf, metrics = metric_set(rmse))
RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(svm_results_rbf, metric = "rmse", n = 1))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "svm-rbf"
cat("SVM RBF complete\n")

# Display results so far
cat("\n=== SVM MODELS COMPLETE ===\n")
print(RMSE %>% select(mean, std_err, name) %>% arrange(mean))

###############################################################################
# PART 4: NEURAL NETWORK
###############################################################################

cat("\n=== STARTING NEURAL NETWORK ===\n")

mlp_recipe <- recipe(traffic_volume ~ ., data = TRAIN.CLEAN) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors())

mlp_model <- mlp(
  hidden_units = tune(),
  penalty = tune(),
  epochs = tune()
) %>%
  set_engine("nnet") %>%
  set_mode("regression")

mlp_workflow <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(mlp_recipe) %>% add_model(mlp_model)

mlp_grid <- grid_regular(
  hidden_units(range = c(15, 35)),
  penalty(range = c(-2, -0.5)),
  epochs(range = c(500, 1000)),
  levels = 3
)

set.seed(542)
mlp_results <- tune_grid(
  mlp_workflow,
  resamples = folds,
  grid = mlp_grid,
  metrics = metric_set(rmse)
)

RMSE <- bind_rows(RMSE, show_best(mlp_results, metric = "rmse", n = 1))
RMSE$name[nrow(RMSE)] <- "nnet"
cat("Neural network complete\n")

# Display results so far
cat("\n=== NEURAL NETWORK COMPLETE ===\n")
print(RMSE %>% select(mean, std_err, name) %>% arrange(mean))


# Your best models
best_boosted <- show_best(boosted_results, metric = "rmse", n = 1)
best_forest <- show_best(forest_results, metric = "rmse", n = 1)
```
